what_it_does: Defines a grouped completion-criteria checklist for a session-recall, lens, synthesis, and channel-context system, with dual FUNCTION/FORM verification rules, status tags, dependency ordering, and a check-back protocol.
observations: Verification requires BOTH a FUNCTION bar (verified by actual use) and a FORM bar (projected, navigable UI surface); code-reading alone is not enough.
The plan is split into eight groups: chunking, recall substrate, lenses, retrieve-then-synthesize panel, channel management/context, multi-project addressing, UI projection, and fork-fleet spin-up.
Many items are [D] or [P]; only a subset of the substrate and lenses are marked [V].
Specific served endpoints are named: :8007 pplx-4b embeddings, :8008 jina-v3 rerank, and several chat-model ports for extract/judge roles.
A license warning notes jina-v3 is CC-BY-NC and must be swapped for commercial use.
Group 6 (multi-project addressing) is explicitly a hard gate: no fleet indexing until Tim picks hierarchy vs lattice.
Group 3.7 requirements/preferences is framed as the keystone new lens and converged with recollection's Pillar-1 identity layer.
Group 4 synthesis panel is built on top of an existing runtime/cognition.py role system, adding new extract/judge roles rather than new infra.
The check-back protocol demands evidence and regression checks before flipping [D] to [V].
standouts: The FUNCTION + FORM dual-bar rule, with the explicit statement that a correct JSON dump is not done for operator-facing surfaces.
Group 6 being a Tim-gated hard gate that blocks all deeper fleet indexing.
Group 3.7 anti-recency preference lens feeding the single Pillar-1 identity layer.
The high proportion of [P] pending-joint and [TIM-GATED] items, making the document more decision log than finished plan.
conventions: Uses a metadata code block at the top with trust/author/date/verified fields.
Labels every criterion with a bracketed status tag inline.
Organizes work into numbered groups with hierarchical sub-items.
References companion documents and prior session notes by relative path and wiki-style link.
Lists dependencies in priority order before the check-back protocol.
States honest limits explicitly (e.g., 'Honest limit, characterized').
concerns: Many [V] claims reference 'today' state without embedded evidence links, so they may drift.
A large share of the plan is gated on Tim/lead reactions, risking staleness if those decisions are slow.
The 'INFERRED-from-Tim proposals' trust note could introduce unverified assumptions.
FORM criteria for Group 7 remain vague because they depend on Tim pointing to UI-building sessions.
notes: A status-tagged rubric that forces both working behavior and projected UI form before any item can be called done, while surfacing what is blocked on Tim or the lead lane.
questions: What exactly counts as a 'projection of addressed state' in the Company UI (The Heart)?
How will the freshness guard compare source mtime/size to the index in practice?
What is the precise file-level boundary between this fork and the lead lane?
What are the exact schema fields for the facet registry in Group 4.3?
How is the default_recall config row set and persisted?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to be Tim's check-back artifact covering his whole vision; the file delivers a detailed, grouped, status-tagged completion checklist with dependency ordering, matching that purpose.
apparent_themes: Verify by use, not by reading code.
Form is half of done; operator-facing surfaces must be visual/spatial projections.
intention_signals: To create a single source of truth that prevents premature 'done' claims by requiring both functional proof and UI form, and to make every blocking decision visible to Tim and the lead lane.
novelty: The explicit two-bar FUNCTION/FORM verification with the rule that a correct but unprojected result is not done, and the family of interpretive lenses built over a channel-memory substrate.
contribution: A structured completion rubric that preserves verification rules, dependency order, status tags, and Tim-gated decisions for the session-recall and channel-context system.
summary_for_embedding: A grouped completion-criteria document for session recall, lenses, synthesis, and channel-context, requiring dual FUNCTION/FORM verification and tagging each item as Verified, Designed, Broken, or Pending-joint.
intention_for_embedding: To serve as Tim's check-back artifact for tracking what is truly complete versus designed, blocked, or gated across the channel-memory plan.
